RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 103-BA, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Ray Reed proudly serves as the State Representative for Missouri’s 83rd House District, which includes vibrant communities across the St. Louis region, including Brentwood, Maplewood, Richmond Heights, Shrewsbury, and portions of Affton. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
Born and raised in Missouri, Ray is a proud Brentwood Eagle, and University of Central Missouri Mule— Ray’s journey in public service began as an intern in the Office of Governor Jay Nixon. There, he worked on critical initiatives, including reviewing legislation, and clemency applications, while assisting with boards and commissions appointments. This early experience gave him a deep understanding of how government can deliver meaningful change when it’s focused on the needs of the people.
Ray’s work centers on creating opportunities for working families, expanding access to affordable healthcare, protecting reproductive rights, and ensuring every Missourian—regardless of their background—can thrive.
Ray Reed’s vision for Missouri is one of hope, progress, and unity— A vision that ensures the best days for Missouri families are still ahead.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Creates provisions relating to professional licenses issued to DACA recipients
Establishes an income tax credit based on the number and ages of a taxpayer's dependent children

Establishes the "Missouri Business Tax Adjustment Act", adjusting the income tax rates applied to the Missouri taxable income of certain business entities based on annual net revenue, upon voter approval
Abolishes the death penalty and specifies that any person sentenced to death must be sentenced to life imprisonment without parole
Modifies existing provisions and creates new provisions regarding cooperation with federal authorities on immigration matters and training for law enforcement agencies
Prioritizes the use of organized labor for state contracts in certain industries
Prohibits discriminatory school or college mascots
Urges the general assembly to advance an agenda for a Missouri Green New Deal
Urges the general assembly to advance an agenda for environmental protection
Inducts Claire McCaskill into the Hall of Famous Missourians

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Authorizes the "Child Care Contribution Tax Credit Act", the "Employer-Provided Child Care Assistance Tax Credit Act", and the "Child Care Providers Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for child care
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Creates provisions relating to insurance coverage for fertility treatments
Creates provisions for automatic voter registration
Modifies provisions relating to railroad safety
Modifies provisions relating to the age of marriage
